purpose: Production contract for the first three motion tests (Archer attack, Slime move, Golem attack) so animation frames can be produced without guessing timing, frame counts, anchors, or file formats. No artwork, animation, or runtime integration is produced by this contract itself.

referenceDesignApproval.note: User approval covers the reference DESIGNS only. No generated frame, sheet, or animation becomes canonical until explicit exact-file approval; canonicalApproved stays false.

runtimeReference.eventMarkerSemantics: Normalized 0..1 time within the state's play duration, consumed by the Asset & Animation Framework (PR #23) as VISUAL cue markers only. Markers never delay, advance, or replace Combat timing or damage.

common.consistentCanvasPerAsset: every frame of one asset's motion test uses the identical canvas size, aspect, character scale, and camera framing
common.noCropping: no frame may crop the silhouette, weapon, bow, quiver, horn, crystal, limbs, or cape — full body plus held equipment stays inside the canvas with a safety margin in every frame
common.cameraLock: no camera movement, no zoom, no framing or perspective change between frames; the 3/4 front view of the approved reference design is fixed for all frames
common.motionBlurPolicy: no motion blur that breaks silhouette readability; small painted smear accents are allowed only if the body silhouette stays fully readable at reduced mobile size
common.referenceDesignLock: costume, colors, weapon, face, hair, and body proportion must match the user-approved reference design exactly in every frame — no redesign, recolor, or proportion drift mid-animation
common.anchorLock: the configured foot anchor stays fixed at the same normalized canvas position in every frame; feet/ground contact must not drift horizontally or vertically except as an explicit key-pose lift that returns to the anchor
common.facing: single-direction art matching the reference design; runtime handles flipX (per Production Pack v2) — never produce mirrored duplicate frames
common.frameNumbering: zero-padded three-digit frameIndex starting at 000 (e.g. archer_attack_000.png)

metadataFormat.eventMarkersField: array of { name, normalizedTime } using only the eventMarkerVocabulary names
metadataFormat.rootMotionField: "in-place" or an explicit per-frame offset list; see monster.slime/move rules
metadataFormat.note: one JSON sidecar per motion test; no new schema beyond the fields the PR #23 manifest already understands (fps, frame count, loop, anchor, timing markers)

canvas.runtimeDisplaySizeSeparate: runtime display scale is owned by the PR #23 manifest (world.scale), never by the art file dimensions; working canvas and battle export are separate outputs
canvas.note: The framework does not lock pixel dimensions; sources must be resolution-independent (or produced at the recommended working canvas), then exported to battle frames at <=512 px longest side per the existing pilot integration contract. No binary artwork is created by this contract.

motionTests.summary: One single bow shot: combat idle -> raise bow and nock arrow -> full visible draw -> release -> small recoil/follow-through -> return to the starting pose.
keyPoses: combat_idle_start (matches the approved reference design stance)
draw_full_tension (bow arc and string clearly silhouetted)
motionTests.anticipation: raise + nock + draw phases; the full-tension pose must hold at least one full frame so the draw reads at 12 FPS
motionTests.actionPhase: the release frame — string returns, bow hand stable, arrow visibly gone from the string
motionTests.recovery: small recoil then settle back into the exact starting combat idle pose (clean final frame, no pop against the idle state)

eventMarkers.name: projectileRelease
eventMarkers.meaning: runtime spawns the arrow projectile VFX at this cue; the arrow, trail, and impact are runtime layers and are NEVER baked into the body frames

motionTests.anchorLockNotes: both feet planted for the entire shot; no step, no hop, no horizontal travel
readabilityChecks: bow arc plus drawn string reads at reduced mobile size
release frame is unmistakable in isolation
silhouette identifies 'archer shooting' with no interior color
forbiddenDeviations: no projectile, trail, or impact baked into body frames
no runtime projectile/damage logic added in this round
no costume/color/weapon/face/proportion change from the approved reference design
no foot travel away from the anchor

motionTests.summary: One seamless bouncing hop cycle: squash to prepare -> stretch and lift -> travel apex -> landing contact -> absorb squash -> recover to the rest shape, looping without a pop.
keyPoses: recover_to_rest_shape (frame 0 and the loop seam must match)
motionTests.anticipation: squash_prepare — clear volume-preserving squash before lift-off
motionTests.actionPhase: stretch_lift through travel_apex to landing_contact (the airborne travel phase)
motionTests.recovery: squash_absorb then recover_to_rest_shape so the cycle loops seamlessly

eventMarkers.name: footstepCue
eventMarkers.meaning: landing/ground-contact cue for optional runtime dust or audio hooks; visual-only, no gameplay effect

motionTests.anchorLockNotes: produced IN-PLACE: no net horizontal displacement is baked across the cycle — the body may rise above the anchor during the hop but ground contact returns to the exact anchor position every cycle; if any root motion is authored, it must be declared as explicit per-frame offsets in the sidecar rootMotion field. World-space movement is owned by the runtime (PR #23 framework + game movement), never by the frames.
readabilityChecks: squash-stretch reads clearly at reduced mobile size
volume stays consistent (no melting or growth between frames)
loop seam shows no visible pop when the cycle repeats
body hue stays distinct from moss/floor per the Map 1 visual lock
forbiddenDeviations: no baked horizontal board travel across the cycle
no anchor drift between cycle start and end
no shape, color, or face change from the approved reference design
no attack, splash, or damage visual mixed into the move cycle

motionTests.summary: One heavy melee strike: combat-ready stance -> weighted wind-up shifting arm and torso mass -> arm raise to peak -> clear impact/contact -> heavy follow-through -> recovery back to the ready stance.
keyPoses: weight_shift_windup (torso and oversized forearm visibly load)
arm_raise_peak (held at least one full frame for weight)
motionTests.anticipation: weight_shift_windup + arm_raise_peak — slow, heavy loading that reads as mass at 12 FPS
motionTests.actionPhase: impact_contact — a single unmistakable contact frame
motionTests.recovery: follow_through_weight then a controlled return to the exact ready stance (clean final frame)

eventMarkers.name: impactCue
eventMarkers.meaning: runtime triggers ground-impact VFX (dust/debris/shockwave layers) at this cue; all impact VFX are separate runtime layers and are NEVER baked into the body frames

motionTests.anchorLockNotes: feet stay planted at the anchor; torso lean and arm motion carry the weight — no step and no lunge that moves the ground contact
readabilityChecks: wind-up and impact read as HEAVY at reduced mobile size
impact frame is unmistakable in isolation
stone-plate separation lines stay visible through the swing
silhouette stays distinct from arena masonry per the Map 1 visual lock
forbiddenDeviations: no VFX (dust, debris, shockwave, glow) baked into body frames
no hitbox, damage, stun, or gameplay logic added in this round
no costume/color/proportion change from the approved reference design
no foot travel or lunge away from the anchor
no fast ninja-like swing that contradicts the heavy weight identity

acceptance: exactly these three motion tests and no others are produced in this round
every frame preserves the transparent background
every frame of one test shares the identical canvas and framing
anchors match this contract exactly and do not drift
frame counts fall inside [frameMin, frameMax] with frameTarget as the goal
event markers appear once per test at the declared normalized time
loop tests repeat seamlessly; non-loop tests end on a clean recovery frame
silhouette, costume, colors, and proportions match the approved reference designs
sidecar metadata JSON accompanies each test with the required fields
canonicalApproved remains false until exact files pass visual review in-game
deferred: full six-state production for all pilots
Arena Ruins in-game runtime test of the produced frames
Slime and Golem reference design specification documents
